006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor;
018
019import static jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.concurrent.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
024import java.util.logging.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
028
029import jakarta.servlet.*;
030import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
031
032/**
033 * Response processor that unwraps {@link CompletableFuture} / {@link CompletionStage} return values from
034 * {@code @RestOp}-annotated methods and bridges them to the servlet container's
035 * {@link AsyncContext asynchronous} request lifecycle.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * When a handler returns a future, this processor is the FIRST processor in the default chain
039 * (see {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.config.DefaultConfig}). It detects the {@link CompletionStage}
040 * content, calls {@link HttpServletRequest#startAsync()} on the underlying servlet request, and
041 * registers a {@link CompletionStage#whenComplete(java.util.function.BiConsumer) whenComplete}
042 * callback that:
043 *
044 * <ul>
045 *    <li>On success — installs the unwrapped value via {@link RestResponse#setContent(Object)}, re-runs
046 *       {@link RestContext#processResponse(RestOpSession)} (this processor sees a non-future and falls
047 *       through to {@code SerializedPojoProcessor} et al.), flushes the response, and
048 *       calls {@link AsyncContext#complete()}.
049 *    <li>On failure — runs the throwable through {@link RestContext#convertThrowable(Throwable)} so
050 *       the existing error pipeline (including {@link ThrowableProcessor} and
051 *       {@link ProblemDetailsProcessor}) handles it, then completes the {@code AsyncContext}.
052 * </ul>
053 *
054 * <h5 class='section'>Synchronous fallback</h5>
055 * <p>
056 * In environments where {@link HttpServletRequest#startAsync()} is unsupported (most notably
057 * Juneau's {@code MockServletRequest}, which returns {@code null}), this processor falls back to a
058 * blocking {@link CompletableFuture#get(long, TimeUnit) get(timeout)} on the future and then
059 * returns {@link #RESTART} so the rest of the chain runs synchronously on the unwrapped value.
060 * This keeps the unit-test surface working without requiring a real servlet container while
061 * preserving full {@code AsyncContext} semantics in production.
062 *
063 * <h5 class='section'>Timeout</h5>
064 * <p>
065 * The async timeout is configurable via {@code @Rest(asyncTimeoutMillis)} /
066 * {@code @RestOp(asyncTimeoutMillis)} (default 30s). On timeout the processor cancels the future
067 * with {@code mayInterruptIfRunning=true} and writes a {@link HttpServletResponse#SC_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT}
068 * response.
069 *
070 * <h5 class='section'>Bare {@link Future} rejection</h5>
071 * <p>
072 * Bare {@link Future} return types (anything that is a {@code Future} but not a
073 * {@link CompletionStage}) are rejected with an {@link InternalServerError} —
074 * polling a {@code Future} would block the request thread without any of the cancellation /
075 * cooperation guarantees that {@link CompletableFuture} provides. Handlers that need async dispatch
076 * must return {@link CompletableFuture} or {@link CompletionStage}.
077 *
078 * <h5 class='section'>Completion executor</h5>
079 * <p>
080 * When {@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor="poolName")} or the per-op equivalent is set, this processor
081 * switches from {@code future.whenComplete(callback)} to {@code future.whenCompleteAsync(callback, executor)},
082 * routing the response-handler work through the named {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} bean. The MDC
083 * snapshot (see below) is taken on the request thread <em>before</em> the executor routing so the MDC context
084 * is always available regardless of which thread the executor picks.
085 *
086 * <h5 class='section'>Thread-local caveats</h5>
087 * <p>
088 * Anything carried via {@link ThreadLocal} (SLF4J MDC, security contexts) does NOT survive the
089 * async boundary — the {@code whenComplete} callback typically runs on a different thread than the
090 * one that produced the future. {@code RequestAttributes}, {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolverSession},
091 * and {@link java.util.Locale} are request-scoped and survive correctly.
092 *
093 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
094 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerAsync">Async Response Handling</a>
095 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ResponseProcessors">Response Processors</a>
096 * </ul>
097 *
098 * @since 10.0.0
099 */
100public class AsyncResponseProcessor implements ResponseProcessor {
101
102   private static final Logger LOG = Logger.getLogger(AsyncResponseProcessor.class.getName());
103
104   /**
105    * Request attribute set by this processor when the {@code AsyncContext} path is taken so
106    * downstream lifecycle code (specifically {@link RestOpSession#finish()} and
107    * {@link RestSession#finish()}) can skip the synchronous {@code flushBuffer()} / {@code req.close()}
108    * — the {@code AsyncContext.complete()} call inside the {@code whenComplete} callback is
109    * responsible for committing the response.
110    *
111    * @since 10.0.0
112    */
113   public static final String ATTR_ASYNC_DISPATCH_OWNED = "org.apache.juneau.rest.server.async.dispatchOwned";
114
115   /** Default async timeout in milliseconds when no annotation override is supplied. */
116   public static final long DEFAULT_ASYNC_TIMEOUT_MILLIS = 30_000L;
117
118   @Override /* Overridden from ResponseProcessor */
119   @SuppressWarnings({
120      "java:S3776", // Async dispatch logic is inherently branchy — splitting further hurts readability.
121      "java:S1141"  // Nested try/catch cleanly separates startAsync IllegalStateException recovery from cancellation.
122   })
123   public int process(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException {
124      var res = opSession.getResponse();
125      var content = res.getContent().orElse(null);
126
127      if (content == null)
128         return NEXT;
129
130      if (content instanceof CompletionStage<?> stage)
131         return processAsync(opSession, stage);
132
133      if (content instanceof Future<?>) {
134         throw new InternalServerError(
135            "Bare java.util.concurrent.Future is not supported as a return type from @RestOp methods. "
136               + "Return CompletableFuture or CompletionStage instead, or block on the result yourself before returning."
137         );
138      }
139
140      return NEXT;
141   }
142
143   private int processAsync(RestOpSession opSession, CompletionStage<?> stage) throws BasicHttpException {
144      var timeoutMs = resolveTimeoutMillis(opSession);
145      var cf = stage.toCompletableFuture();
146      var req = opSession.getRequest().getHttpServletRequest();
147
148      // True async path requires both isAsyncSupported() and a non-null AsyncContext from startAsync().
149      AsyncContext asyncCtx = null;
150      if (req.isAsyncSupported()) {
151         try {
152            asyncCtx = req.startAsync();
153            if (asyncCtx != null && timeoutMs > 0)
154               asyncCtx.setTimeout(timeoutMs);
155         } catch (IllegalStateException e) {
156            asyncCtx = null;  // Already committed or async not actually supported on this request.
157         }
158      }
159
160      if (asyncCtx == null)
161         return processSyncFallback(opSession, cf, timeoutMs);
162
163      dispatchAsync(opSession, cf, asyncCtx, timeoutMs);
164      return FINISHED;
165   }
166
167   @SuppressWarnings({
168      "java:S2142", // We re-set the interrupt flag immediately and surface as a 500.
169      "java:S1166", // CancellationException is intentionally swallowed; we surface it as a 500 via convertThrowable.
170      "java:S3516"  // Always returns RESTART by design: every outcome (success or error) re-runs the chain on the now-synchronous content.
171   })
172   private int processSyncFallback(RestOpSession opSession, CompletableFuture<?> cf, long timeoutMs) {
173      var res = opSession.getResponse();
174      try {
175         Object value = (timeoutMs > 0) ? cf.get(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS) : cf.get();
176         res.setContent(value);
177         return RESTART;
178      } catch (TimeoutException te) {
179         cf.cancel(true);
180         res.setStatus(SC_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT);
181         res.setContent(opSession.getRestContext().convertThrowable(
182            new GatewayTimeout("Async response timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms.")
183         ));
184         return RESTART;
185      } catch (CancellationException ce) {
186         res.setStatus(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);
187         res.setContent(opSession.getRestContext().convertThrowable(ce));
188         return RESTART;
189      } catch (ExecutionException ee) {
190         Throwable cause = ee.getCause() == null ? ee : ee.getCause();
191         res.setStatus(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);
192         res.setContent(opSession.getRestContext().convertThrowable(cause));
193         return RESTART;
194      } catch (InterruptedException ie) {
195         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
196         cf.cancel(true);
197         res.setStatus(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);
198         res.setContent(opSession.getRestContext().convertThrowable(ie));
199         return RESTART;
200      }
201   }
202
203   private void dispatchAsync(RestOpSession opSession, CompletableFuture<?> cf, AsyncContext asyncCtx, long timeoutMs) {
204      var req = opSession.getRequest().getHttpServletRequest();
205      req.setAttribute(ATTR_ASYNC_DISPATCH_OWNED, Boolean.TRUE);
206
207      var done = new AtomicBoolean(false);
208
209      asyncCtx.addListener(new AsyncListener() {
210         @Override public void onComplete(AsyncEvent ev) { /* no-op */ }
211         @Override public void onStartAsync(AsyncEvent ev) { /* no-op */ }
212         @Override public void onError(AsyncEvent ev) {
213            finalizeAsync(opSession, cf, asyncCtx, ev.getThrowable(), done, /* timeout */ false, timeoutMs);
214         }
215         @Override public void onTimeout(AsyncEvent ev) {
216            finalizeAsync(opSession, cf, asyncCtx, null, done, /* timeout */ true, timeoutMs);
217         }
218      });
219
220      // MDC bridge: snapshot request-thread MDC at dispatch time, then restore it on the
221      // completion thread so log statements inside the whenComplete callback see the same diagnostic
222      // context (requestId, userId, etc.) that was set by upstream filters.
223      // NOTE: the MDC wrap happens BEFORE the executor routing so the snapshot is always
224      // available regardless of which thread the executor picks.
225      var mdcSnapshot = opSession.getRestContext().isMdcAsyncPropagation()
226         ? MdcAsyncListener.snapshot()
227         : null;
228
229      var callback = MdcAsyncListener.wrap(
230         (value, error) -> finalizeAsync(opSession, cf, asyncCtx, error, done, /* timeout */ false, timeoutMs, value),
231         mdcSnapshot
232      );
233
234      // If a per-op or resource-level completion executor is configured, route the callback
235      // through it via whenCompleteAsync; otherwise use the natural completion thread.
236      var executor = opSession.getContext().getAsyncCompletionExecutor();
237      if (executor != null)
238         cf.whenCompleteAsync(callback, executor);
239      else
240         cf.whenComplete(callback);
241   }
242
243   private void finalizeAsync(RestOpSession opSession, CompletableFuture<?> cf, AsyncContext asyncCtx,
244         Throwable error, AtomicBoolean done, boolean timeout, long timeoutMs) {
245      finalizeAsync(opSession, cf, asyncCtx, error, done, timeout, timeoutMs, null);
246   }
247
248   @SuppressWarnings({
249      "java:S3776", // Async finalization is inherently branchy.
250      "java:S1141", // Nested try/catch separates response-processing from container cleanup.
251      "java:S107"   // The 8 parameters are the internal async-finalization context (session, future, async ctx, error, done-latch, timeout flag, timeout ms, resolved value); a holder object would obscure this hot-path call and its sibling overload.
252   })
253   private void finalizeAsync(RestOpSession opSession, CompletableFuture<?> cf, AsyncContext asyncCtx,
254         Throwable error, AtomicBoolean done, boolean timeout, long timeoutMs, Object value) {
255      if (! done.compareAndSet(false, true))
256         return;
257
258      var res = opSession.getResponse();
259
260      try {
261         if (timeout) {
262            cf.cancel(true);
263            res.setStatus(SC_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT);
264            res.setContent(opSession.getRestContext().convertThrowable(
265               new GatewayTimeout("Async response timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms.")
266            ));
267         } else if (error != null) {
268            Throwable cause = unwrap(error);
269            res.setStatus(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);
270            res.setContent(opSession.getRestContext().convertThrowable(cause));
271         } else {
272            res.setContent(value);
273         }
274
275         opSession.getRestContext().processResponse(opSession);
276         res.flushBuffer();
277      } catch (Exception e) {
278         LOG.log(Level.WARNING, e, () -> "Async response finalization failed: " + e.getMessage());
279         try {
280            if (! res.getHttpServletResponse().isCommitted())
281               res.getHttpServletResponse().sendError(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);
282         } catch (Exception inner) {
283            LOG.log(Level.FINEST, inner, () -> "Async response error-fallback also failed: " + inner.getMessage());
284         }
285      } finally {
286         try {
287            asyncCtx.complete();
288         } catch (IllegalStateException ise) {
289            LOG.log(Level.FINEST, ise, () -> "AsyncContext.complete() raced with the container: " + ise.getMessage());
290         }
291      }
292   }
293
294   private static Throwable unwrap(Throwable t) {
295      if (t instanceof CompletionException && t.getCause() != null)
296         return t.getCause();
297      if (t instanceof ExecutionException && t.getCause() != null)
298         return t.getCause();
299      return t;
300   }
301
302   private static long resolveTimeoutMillis(RestOpSession opSession) {
303      var op = opSession.getContext().getAsyncTimeoutMillis();
304      if (op > 0)
305         return op;
306      var ctx = opSession.getRestContext().getAsyncTimeoutMillis();
307      return ctx > 0 ? ctx : DEFAULT_ASYNC_TIMEOUT_MILLIS;
308   }
309
310   /**
311    * Returns whether the given session has been handed off to async dispatch. Inspected by
312    * {@link RestOpSession#finish()} / {@link RestSession#finish()} to skip the synchronous
313    * {@code flushBuffer()} call when the {@code AsyncContext.complete()} path will commit the
314    * response itself.
315    *
316    * @param opSession The session to check.
317    * @return {@code true} if {@link #ATTR_ASYNC_DISPATCH_OWNED} has been set.
318    */
319   public static boolean isAsyncDispatchOwned(RestOpSession opSession) {
320      if (opSession == null)
321         return false;
322      return isAsyncDispatchOwned(opSession.getRequest().getHttpServletRequest());
323   }
324
325   /**
326    * Variant of {@link #isAsyncDispatchOwned(RestOpSession)} that operates directly on the
327    * underlying servlet request — used by {@link RestSession#finish()} where the
328    * {@link RestOpSession} may not be available.
329    *
330    * @param req The servlet request.
331    * @return {@code true} if {@link #ATTR_ASYNC_DISPATCH_OWNED} has been set.
332    */
333   public static boolean isAsyncDispatchOwned(HttpServletRequest req) {
334      if (req == null)
335         return false;
336      return Boolean.TRUE.equals(req.getAttribute(ATTR_ASYNC_DISPATCH_OWNED));
337   }
338}
